name: Sukku Malli Coffee
description: Sukku Malli Coffee, also known as Chukku Kaapi or Dry Ginger Coffee, is a traditional South Indian herbal beverage made from dried ginger, coriander seeds, black pepper and cardamom. It is a soothing, aromatic drink with numerous health benefits — it aids digestion, boosts immunity, relieves cold and cough, and improves blood circulation. A popular alternative to regular tea or coffee, it is especially comforting during rainy seasons and winter months.

steps.instruction: Rinse dried ginger well and dry it. Peel the skin and discard.
Using a mortar and pestle, roughly crush the dried ginger into smaller pieces.
steps.tip: Crush dried ginger roughly before grinding — adding whole pieces can damage your mixer.

steps.instruction: To a dry pan, add coriander seeds, black pepper and cardamom pods.
Dry roast on low-medium flame until golden and aromatic. Do not burn.
steps.tip: Dry roast spices on low-medium flame to preserve aroma and prevent burning.

steps.instruction: Add the crushed dry ginger and give a quick sauté. Switch off.
Add dried tulsi leaves and nutmeg powder towards the end. Roast for another 30 seconds.
Cool completely, then transfer to a mixer jar.
steps.tip: Cool ingredients completely before grinding to prevent moisture in the powder.

steps.instruction: Grind to a fine powder. Cool down completely.
Store in an airtight container in a dry place. Use within one month for best flavour.
steps.tip: The powder can be stored for up to a month in an airtight container.

steps.instruction: To prepare coffee without milk: Boil 1 cup water in a saucepan. Add 1 tsp Sukku Malli powder and 1 tbsp palm jaggery.
Let it simmer for 2 to 3 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the remains. Serve hot.
To prepare coffee with milk: Boil ½ cup water and ½ cup milk together. Add 1 tsp Sukku Malli powder.
Let it come to a rolling boil, simmer for 1 minute.
Add 1 tbsp palm jaggery. Switch off.
steps.tip: Palm sugar candy (panangarkandu) is the best sweetener for cold and cough relief.
Sukku Malli Coffee is best consumed warm for maximum health benefits.

tips: Crush dried ginger roughly before grinding — adding whole pieces can damage your mixer.
Dry roast spices on low-medium flame to preserve aroma and prevent burning.
Cool ingredients completely before grinding to prevent moisture in the powder.
Palm sugar candy (panangarkandu) is the best sweetener for cold and cough relief.
You can use karupatti (palm jaggery), regular jaggery or even honey (add only to lukewarm coffee).
Add a cardamom pod while boiling for extra aroma.
Sukku Malli Coffee is best consumed warm for maximum health benefits.
The powder can be stored for up to a month in an airtight container.
